channel.title: HoopsHype Rumors - Cleveland Cavaliers
channel.description: Cleveland Cavaliers trade rumors and free agency buzz from HoopsHype

item.title: Players with 4,000+ playoff points and 0 rings: — Karl …
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] StatMuse: Players with 4,000+ playoff points and 0 rings: — Karl Malone — James Harden That's it. x.com

item.title: Evan Mobley is the first EC player since Michael …
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] StatMuse: Evan Mobley is the first EC player since Michael Jordan with 5+ assists 3+ steals 5+ blocks in a playoff game. x.com

item.title: "I apologized to the group," he said following the …
item.description: It also came after he addressed his teammates in the locker room at halftime. "I apologized to the group," he said following the series-evening win. "I set the tone in Game 3, getting downhill in transition, and I don't think I did that one time in the first half. And for me, that's the catalyst. It's not always just the scoring, right? Like I was able to get into the paint and score. Trying to just at least create confusion, trying to set the tone early in the offense. I didn't do that in the f

item.title: JB Bickerstaff criticizes officiating: ‘Ever since we came to Cleveland, the whistle has changed’
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: JB Bickerstaff on the 34-12 foul disparity: “It’s unacceptable. It is. We didn’t do enough obviously to help ourselves and I’ll start there, but ever since we came to Cleveland the whistle has changed.”

item.title: Donovan Mitchell demolishes Pistons in the second half to even series
item.description: Danny Cunningham: #Cavs beat the Pistons 112-103 in a game that wasn't as close as the final score. Donovan Mitchell had one of his best games as a member of the Cavs, finishing with 43 points -- 39 of them in the 2nd half. Harden had 24 and hit five 3s. Series is tied at 2-2.

item.title: Donovan Mitchell ties Sleepy Floyd’s record for most points in a playoff half
item.description: Tim Reynolds: Most points in a playoff half ... 39 - Donovan Mitchell (2nd), tonight; 39 - Sleepy Floyd (2nd), 1987; 38 - Charles Barkley (1st), 1994; 38 - Kevin Durant (1st), 2019

item.title: Game 4 vs. Detroit marks Donovan Mitchell's fourth …
item.description: ESPN Insight: Game 4 vs. Detroit marks Donovan Mitchell's fourth 40-point playoff game with the Cavaliers, passing Kyrie Irving for second-most in franchise history . Only LeBron James (24) has more.

item.title: The @cavs' 24-0 run was the longest in an @NBA playoff …
item.description: Cavs Notes: The @cavs' 24-0 run was the longest in an @NBA playoff game since Minnesota scored 24 straight on May 16, 2024 against DEN. It was the longest run in a playoff game by Cleveland since 1997-98. The team’s previous long over that span was 19 straight on May 16, 2008 vs. BOS.

item.title: Congrats to @James Harden of the @cavs for moving up …
item.description: NBA: Congrats to @James Harden of the @cavs for moving up to 7th on the all-time playoff STEALS list!

item.title: Thomas Bryant is available tonight
item.description: Danny Cunningham: #Cavs big man Thomas Bryant is available tonight.

item.title: The Cavaliers have listed Thomas Bryant (low back …
item.description: The Cavaliers have listed Thomas Bryant (low back spasms) as questionable for Monday's Game 4 against Detroit.

item.title: Atkinson and Blair played on the same college …
item.description: But Atkinson, the Cavaliers head coach, largely steered clear. In the fourth quarter with the game on the line, Blair called a loose-ball foul on the Cavs and Atkinson hated the call, but rather than scream or dart toward the official, Atkinson just sort of stood near his bench, staring at Blair and shaking his head. There’s history here, and not the usual kind between a coach and a referee. A lifetime ago, Atkinson and Blair played on the same college basketball team. That and $6.85 would be en

item.title: “There is no, I’m not going to say anything to Curtis …
item.description: “You know, listen, there’s no, like, friends, right? We’re competing,” Atkinson told The Athletic in a phone call Saturday after his team’s 116-109 Game 3 win. “There is no, I’m not going to say anything to Curtis because we went to school together. There’s none of that. I would say it’s like super professional, our interactions. But we both know we have this shared history.”

item.title: And yes, from 1988 to 1990, they were teammates for …
item.description: Atkinson, 58, has been on an NBA sideline as an assistant or head coach since 2008. Blair, 55, is in his 18th season as an NBA official, which means they broke into the league at roughly the same time. And yes, from 1988 to 1990, they were teammates for the “giant killer” Richmond Spiders teams of the era, forming a tantalizing backcourt when Atkinson was a senior and Blair a sophomore and the two led the team in scoring.

item.title: “We’re never like, ‘Hey, Curt, remember when you hit …
item.description: Atkinson said he and Blair have never sat down over coffee or a bottle of red to rehash the past, how they got to where they are now, or how to navigate the two. Those who knew them when say neither of them brings it up in phone conversations, either. Kenny coaches. Curtis refs. “We’re never like, ‘Hey, Curt, remember when you hit the buzzer-beater against American University to put us in the finals of the CAA tournament?’ We don’t reminisce,” Atkinson said. “We’re not joking about (their Richmo

item.title: Timberwolves, Cavaliers, Celtics, Knicks, Lakers could pursue Giannis Antetokounmpo depending on how their playoffs go
item.description: The NBA playoffs represent another landmark point for the Antetokounmpo sweepstakes. Sources said postseason teams such as the Minnesota Timberwolves, Cleveland Cavaliers, Boston Celtics, Knicks and Los Angeles Lakers pursued Antetokounmpo at the February deadline, and their finishes to the campaign will play a major factor in their aggressiveness to trade for Antetokounmpo.

item.title: NBA Last Two Minute Report from Game 3 shows 0 errors. …
item.description: Chris Fedor: NBA Last Two Minute Report from Game 3 shows 0 errors. Tobias Harris’ late contest on James Harden’s 3-point dagger was clean, not considered a foul. “Harris jumps to contest Harden's shot, but would otherwise avoid making contact if not for Harden extending forward.”

item.title: Knicks with 18 threes in the first half, tied for the …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Knicks with 18 threes in the first half, tied for the most in a playoff half in NBA history. Most since the Cavs in 2016.

item.title: "I apologized to the group," he said following the …
item.description: It also came after he addressed his teammates in the locker room at halftime. "I apologized to the group," he said following the series-evening win. "I set the tone in Game 3, getting downhill in transition, and I don't think I did that one time in the first half. And for me, that's the catalyst. It's not always just the scoring, right? Like I was able to get into the paint and score. Trying to just at least create confusion, trying to set the tone early in the offense. I didn't do that in the f

item.title: JB Bickerstaff criticizes officiating: ‘Ever since we came to Cleveland, the whistle has changed’
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: JB Bickerstaff on the 34-12 foul disparity: “It’s unacceptable. It is. We didn’t do enough obviously to help ourselves and I’ll start there, but ever since we came to Cleveland the whistle has changed.”

item.title: Donovan Mitchell demolishes Pistons in the second half to even series
item.description: Danny Cunningham: #Cavs beat the Pistons 112-103 in a game that wasn't as close as the final score. Donovan Mitchell had one of his best games as a member of the Cavs, finishing with 43 points -- 39 of them in the 2nd half. Harden had 24 and hit five 3s. Series is tied at 2-2.

item.title: Donovan Mitchell ties Sleepy Floyd’s record for most points in a playoff half
item.description: Tim Reynolds: Most points in a playoff half ... 39 - Donovan Mitchell (2nd), tonight; 39 - Sleepy Floyd (2nd), 1987; 38 - Charles Barkley (1st), 1994; 38 - Kevin Durant (1st), 2019

item.title: Game 4 vs. Detroit marks Donovan Mitchell's fourth …
item.description: ESPN Insight: Game 4 vs. Detroit marks Donovan Mitchell's fourth 40-point playoff game with the Cavaliers, passing Kyrie Irving for second-most in franchise history . Only LeBron James (24) has more.

item.title: The @cavs' 24-0 run was the longest in an @NBA playoff …
item.description: Cavs Notes: The @cavs' 24-0 run was the longest in an @NBA playoff game since Minnesota scored 24 straight on May 16, 2024 against DEN. It was the longest run in a playoff game by Cleveland since 1997-98. The team’s previous long over that span was 19 straight on May 16, 2008 vs. BOS.

item.title: Congrats to @James Harden of the @cavs for moving up …
item.description: NBA: Congrats to @James Harden of the @cavs for moving up to 7th on the all-time playoff STEALS list!

item.title: Thomas Bryant is available tonight
item.description: Danny Cunningham: #Cavs big man Thomas Bryant is available tonight.

item.title: The Cavaliers have listed Thomas Bryant (low back …
item.description: The Cavaliers have listed Thomas Bryant (low back spasms) as questionable for Monday's Game 4 against Detroit.

item.title: Atkinson and Blair played on the same college …
item.description: But Atkinson, the Cavaliers head coach, largely steered clear. In the fourth quarter with the game on the line, Blair called a loose-ball foul on the Cavs and Atkinson hated the call, but rather than scream or dart toward the official, Atkinson just sort of stood near his bench, staring at Blair and shaking his head. There’s history here, and not the usual kind between a coach and a referee. A lifetime ago, Atkinson and Blair played on the same college basketball team. That and $6.85 would be en

item.title: “There is no, I’m not going to say anything to Curtis …
item.description: “You know, listen, there’s no, like, friends, right? We’re competing,” Atkinson told The Athletic in a phone call Saturday after his team’s 116-109 Game 3 win. “There is no, I’m not going to say anything to Curtis because we went to school together. There’s none of that. I would say it’s like super professional, our interactions. But we both know we have this shared history.”

item.title: And yes, from 1988 to 1990, they were teammates for …
item.description: Atkinson, 58, has been on an NBA sideline as an assistant or head coach since 2008. Blair, 55, is in his 18th season as an NBA official, which means they broke into the league at roughly the same time. And yes, from 1988 to 1990, they were teammates for the “giant killer” Richmond Spiders teams of the era, forming a tantalizing backcourt when Atkinson was a senior and Blair a sophomore and the two led the team in scoring.

item.title: “We’re never like, ‘Hey, Curt, remember when you hit …
item.description: Atkinson said he and Blair have never sat down over coffee or a bottle of red to rehash the past, how they got to where they are now, or how to navigate the two. Those who knew them when say neither of them brings it up in phone conversations, either. Kenny coaches. Curtis refs. “We’re never like, ‘Hey, Curt, remember when you hit the buzzer-beater against American University to put us in the finals of the CAA tournament?’ We don’t reminisce,” Atkinson said. “We’re not joking about (their Richmo

item.title: Timberwolves, Cavaliers, Celtics, Knicks, Lakers could pursue Giannis Antetokounmpo depending on how their playoffs go
item.description: The NBA playoffs represent another landmark point for the Antetokounmpo sweepstakes. Sources said postseason teams such as the Minnesota Timberwolves, Cleveland Cavaliers, Boston Celtics, Knicks and Los Angeles Lakers pursued Antetokounmpo at the February deadline, and their finishes to the campaign will play a major factor in their aggressiveness to trade for Antetokounmpo.

item.title: NBA Last Two Minute Report from Game 3 shows 0 errors. …
item.description: Chris Fedor: NBA Last Two Minute Report from Game 3 shows 0 errors. Tobias Harris’ late contest on James Harden’s 3-point dagger was clean, not considered a foul. “Harris jumps to contest Harden's shot, but would otherwise avoid making contact if not for Harden extending forward.”

item.title: Knicks with 18 threes in the first half, tied for the …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Knicks with 18 threes in the first half, tied for the most in a playoff half in NBA history. Most since the Cavs in 2016.

item.title: “Magic, Michael, Kareem, Kobe had killer instincts. …
item.description: Run It Back: “Magic, Michael, Kareem, Kobe had killer instincts. ... I love LeBron, but at times he doesn't bring it the way we want him to bring it, like James Harden." - Tim Hardaway Sr.

item.title: “They’re getting more calls than we are.”
item.description: Hunter Patterson: J.B. when asked how the whistle has changed after Kenny Atkinson’s comments: “They’re getting more calls than we are.”

item.title: “With Cade, you cannot give him the same look. He’s …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Kenny Atkinson on defending Cade: “With Cade, you cannot give him the same look. He’s like a great quarterback, you give the same look to him he’s gonna figure it out. Keep mixing and matching different coverages, different matchups.

item.title: “What’s hard to do is stop momentum. … It shifts back …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Kenny Atkinson on the Cavs having momentum after winning the last two: “What’s hard to do is stop momentum. … It shifts back and forth, but I do like that we’ve caught this momentum and energy. We’ve gotta bring it forward, we’ve gotta carry it over to the road.”

item.title: Cade Cunningham had a game-high 39 points on 6/10 from …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Cavs hold on to beat the Pistons 117-113 after outscoring them 14-10 in overtime. Cleveland takes a 3-2 series lead. Cade Cunningham had a game-high 39 points on 6/10 from distance, 9 assists, 7 boards and 2 steals.

item.title: Pistons led by 9 with 2:37 left in the 4th quarter
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Pistons led by 9 with 2:37 left in the 4th quarter. They were outscored 23-10 the through the end of the 4th & OT, shooting 4-for-17 from the field.

item.title: Cade Cunningham: 'We all saw the play, I think it’s pretty clear it was a foul'
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Cade Cunningham on the final play of regulation: “We all saw the play, I think it’s pretty clear it was a foul. (Ausar) has the ball in his possession, he’s running forward, he gets tripped up. It’s a foul, it’s been a foul the whole rest of the game. Wasn’t a foul that time.”

item.title: On the late no-call against Ausar: “He fouled Ausar. …
item.description: ?Omari Sankofa II?: JB: “They started doubling and getting the ball out of Cade’s hands. We’ll find ways to fix it. We’ll be better.” On the late no-call against Ausar: “He fouled Ausar. It’s clear. He trips him when he’s going for a loose ball. End of game situation, that’s tough.”

item.title: Cleveland outscored Detroit 23-10, shot 50% to the …
item.description: ?Kurt Helin?: In the final 3 minutes and OT, Cleveland outscored Detroit 23-10, shot 50% to the Pistons 23.5%, was 2-of-3 from 3 while Detroit was 0-of-5, and Mitchell was 4-of-5 with 9 points, while Cunningham was 1-of-4 for 2 points.

item.title: The former NBA Most Valuable Player became just the …
item.description: The former NBA Most Valuable Player became just the fourth player in the history of the Association to score at least 4,000 points, collect 1,000 rebounds and dish out 1,000 dimes in the playoffs. Two of the players to have done it are in the Basketball Hall of Fame, while the other is a lock to be inducted into Springfield.

item.title: “Just sticking with it. Every single minute, every …
item.description: Evan Mobley emphasized composure and collective execution after the Cleveland Cavaliers escaped with a 117-113 overtime win over the Detroit Pistons in Game 5 of the Eastern Conference semifinals. “Just sticking with it. Every single minute, every single second counts at the end of games like this and I think we stuck with it till the very end,” Mobley said, describing the mindset behind Cleveland’s comeback from a nine-point deficit in the final minutes of regulation. The Cavaliers turned a 103

item.title: Pistons got Evan Mobley looking like Scar
item.description: ?Spencer Davies?: Pistons got Evan Mobley looking like Scar.

item.title: Max Strus: "I look better? You're saying I look …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Max Strus: "I look better? You're saying I look bigger?" "I do. I think you look like the Pistons. They got a bunch of big guys. I think you look like them" Max: "I'll take that as a compliment" ??

item.title: "That's a pretty man. He's a good looking man. I would …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Chuck on Max Strus: "That's a pretty man. He's a good looking man. I would not look him dead in the eyes. I made that mistake with Tom Brady one time. He's a better shooter than Tom Brady" ??
